make a comparison of shortwave infrared camera, medium wave infrared camera, longwave infrared camera in terms of their product characteristics, functionality, and application fields.

Shortwave infrared cameras, medium wave infrared cameras, and longwave infrared cameras are all types of thermal imaging cameras with varying product characteristics, functionality, and application fields. 

Product Characteristics:
- Shortwave infrared cameras have a wavelength range of 0.9-1.7 microns and can detect small temperature differences as low as 0.02°C. 
- Medium wave infrared cameras have a wavelength range of 3-5 microns and have high sensitivity to temperature differences of 0.1-0.2°C. 
- Longwave infrared cameras have a wavelength range of 7-14 microns and can detect temperature differences as low as 0.05°C. 

Functionality:
- Shortwave infrared cameras are useful for sensing through obscurants like fog, dust, and smoke as they can penetrate through these materials. Additionally, they can detect changes in the vibrational state of molecules, making them useful for material analysis and identifying objects in difficult environments.
- Medium wave infrared cameras can detect gases or vapors by their absorption of light at specific wavelengths, making them ideal for applications in industries such as environmental monitoring, chemical processing, and petrochemicals. 
- Longwave infrared cameras are used for detecting heat signatures and can be useful in imaging of electrical components and mechanical parts, as well as detecting humans or animals in security and surveillance applications. 

Application Fields:
- Shortwave infrared cameras are often used in the military, chemical processing, semiconductor manufacturing, and surveillance.
- Medium wave infrared cameras can be used in oil and gas, environmental monitoring, and food processing industries, among others. 
- Longwave infrared cameras are widely used in building inspections, electrical and mechanical inspections, and medical imaging. 

Overall, selecting between the three types of cameras depends on the specific application requirements, environmental conditions and budget of the user.

edit and expand this paragraph with a more professional and plain tone: Here at Lano Photonics we have a few categories of infrared cameras: 1. short wave infrared camera. 2. Medium wave infrared camera. 3. Long wave infrared camera. 4. Handheld thermal camera

At Lano Photonics, we offer several categories of infrared cameras designed for various applications. These cameras include the Short Wave Infrared Camera, Medium Wave Infrared Camera, Long Wave Infrared Camera, and Handheld Thermal Camera. 

Our Short Wave Infrared Camera is designed to operate in the range of 0.9-1.7 microns. It is highly sensitive, and can accurately detect even the smallest temperature differences of up to 0.02°C. This camera is particularly useful for applications that require sensing through obscurants such as fog, dust, or smoke. 

The Medium Wave Infrared Camera, on the other hand, operates in the range of 3-5 microns and has a high sensitivity to temperature differences of 0.1-0.2°C. Its ability to detect gases or vapors by their absorption of light at specific wavelengths makes it ideal for applications in environmental monitoring, chemical processing, and petrochemicals.

Our Long Wave Infrared Camera operates in the range of 7-14 microns and is capable of detecting temperature differences as low as 0.05°C. This camera is extensively used in applications such as building inspections, electrical and mechanical inspections, and medical imaging. 

Finally, our Handheld Thermal Camera is designed for portable, on-the-go use, and is ideal for applications such as outdoor fieldwork, home inspections, and emergency response. 

At Lano Photonics, we are committed to providing innovative, high-quality infrared cameras that meet the unique needs of our customers. Our team of experienced professionals is dedicated to delivering unmatched service, and we pride ourselves on our ability to provide tailored solutions that exceed our clients' expectations.

The existing models are LANO-SC-50C/U; LANO-SC130-91G; LANO-SWLI51-10CL; LANO-SWLIR51-20CL; LANO-SWIR51-101G. 2. Product details: Multi-resolution: 512 X 1 pixel @ 1024 X 1 pixel. A variety of image processing algorithms, and a variety of external trigger modes. easy to use. Data interface: CameraLink/GigE Maximum line frame: 10KHZ & 20KHz Support product customization. 3. Briefly mention the application field. 4. make a summary of the abovementioned details.

Shortwave infrared cameras are an innovative solution for detecting thermal radiation and producing high-quality images. At Lano Photonics, we offer a range of shortwave infrared camera models, including the LANO-SC-50C/U, LANO-SC130-91G, LANO-SWLI51-10CL, LANO-SWLIR51-20CL, and LANO-SWIR51-101G. Each of these models is designed to provide unmatched accuracy and reliability, making them a top choice for a wide range of applications.

Our shortwave infrared cameras are equipped with a variety of features, including multi-resolution capabilities of 512 X 1 pixel @ 1024 X 1 pixel. They also boast exceptional image processing algorithms and a range of external trigger modes, making them extremely user-friendly and easy to navigate. Our cameras also offer a data interface option of either CameraLink or GigE and boast maximum line frame rates of 10KHz & 20KHz.

In addition to these powerful features, our shortwave infrared cameras are also highly customizable, which means they can be tailored to meet the unique needs of customers across a range of industries. They are commonly used in a variety of applications, including industrial automation, semiconductor inspection, and environmental monitoring.

In summary, if you are looking for a high-quality shortwave infrared camera that delivers exceptional accuracy and reliability, Lano Photonics is the ideal partner for you. Our shortwave infrared cameras are designed with your specific needs in mind, boasting powerful features and a range of customization options to suit your unique requirements. With our cameras, you can be confident in your ability to capture accurate, high-quality images that will help you achieve your goals and advance your business.
